findings.title: New-Trip modal: 'Trip name', 'Destination' & template-code inputs have no programmatic label (screen reader announces blank fields)
findings.rootCause: The `<label class="form-label">` elements for trip name (line 48) and destination (line 52) carry NO `for`/`htmlFor` attribute and do not wrap the input, so there is no programmatic association; the inputs (#tripName, #tripPlaceInput) also have no `aria-label`. The template-code `<input id="templateCodeInput">` (line 41) has only a placeholder. By contrast the date label on line 62 correctly uses `for="tripDateRange"`, so the pattern was known but not applied to the text inputs. The route-level axe gate (tests/e2e/a11y.spec.js) never opens this modal, so the violation is uncaught.

findings.title: Edit-Trip modal: 'Adventure name' & 'Destination' inputs have unassociated labels (same label gap as New-Trip)
findings.rootCause: `<label class="form-label">` on lines 253, 257 (and 277) have no `for` and don't wrap their inputs (#editTripName, #editTripPlaceInput, #editTripCoverInput); inputs carry no `aria-label`. Only the date label (line 264) uses `for=`. WCAG 1.3.1 / 4.1.2 (label / button-name) fail. Uncaught because the route-only axe gate never opens this modal.

findings.title: Add-Day modal: 'Where' and 'Date' inputs have labels with no `for` association
findings.rootCause: `<label class="form-label text-black/50">` on lines 56 and 60 have no `for`; the inputs (#dayName line 57, #dayDate line 61) have ids but no matching label association and no `aria-label`. The inputs already carry ids, so the fix is a one-line `for=` add on each label. Not covered by the route-only axe gate (modal isn't opened during the scan).

findings.title: Home map search input has no accessible name (placeholder is not a label)
findings.rootCause: `<input id="homeMapSearchInput" type="text" ... placeholder={t('home.searchMapPlaceholder')}>` has no `aria-label` and no associated `<label>`. Placeholder text is not an accessible name (WCAG 4.1.2 / 1.3.1). The adjacent magnifier SVG is `aria-hidden`. The route axe gate scans #home but MapSearchBar renders only inside TripView (requires a seeded trip), so a trip-less fresh-app scan won't flag it.

findings.title: Map-search results dropdown is not announced (no aria-live / combobox semantics) and offers no arrow-key navigation
findings.rootCause: `#homeMapSearchResults` (MapSearchBar.tsx:68) is a plain `<div>` with no `role="listbox"`/`aria-live`; mapSearch.ts populates it via `resultsEl.innerHTML` (lines 105, 109) with rows rendered as bare `<button class="map-search-row">` (line 119-128) — no `role="option"`, no `aria-activedescendant`, no aria-expanded on the input, and the input has no keydown handler for ArrowUp/Down/Enter (only an `input` listener + a results-container click handler at line 224). The 'No matches.' message (line 105) is therefore silent to assistive tech.

findings.title: Day-modal shortlist AM/PM/Eve toggle buttons: no aria-pressed and their labels are overwritten with hardcoded English
findings.rootCause: The buttons are rendered with `title` only (line 118-123) and no `aria-pressed`, even though they are stateful toggles (the title flips between add/remove). `refreshShortlistButtons()` then sets `btn.textContent = isThere ? '✓ ' + label : label` where `label = time === 'morning' ? '🌅 AM' : ...` (lines 747-748) — hardcoded English, ignoring the localized `_slotLabel`/`t()` strings used elsewhere in the same modal. So the toggle state is invisible to AT, and the i18n'd glyph+label regresses to English on refresh.

findings.title: LoginWall: button-ready transition is not announced; only the loading/stalled states use aria-live
findings.rootCause: The loading and stalled hints are `aria-live="polite"` (lines 143, 149), but the 'ready' path (status==='ready', line 78) just unmounts the loading region and injects Google's GIS-rendered button into #loginWallBtnContainer; nothing announces the state change. Removing a live region does not produce an announcement. The freshly rendered Google button also receives no focus.

findings.title: HeroMap place InfoWindow action buttons/link are hardcoded English ('Add to to-do list', 'View on Google Maps') — not localized
findings.rootCause: buildInfoWindowHtml() hardcodes the button text on line 288 and the anchor text on line 302 as English string literals instead of using t(). This is the integration seam where a POI/search result is added to the to-do list, so the only label on that action is non-localized.
